objective: Enumerate privilege escalation vectors from jonathan (uid=1000) on Snapped (10.129.9.2). Report findings — do not attempt exploitation.

current_access.notes: Stable SSH shell. Only member of group jonathan. Nginx UI v2.3.2 runs on the box — check what user it runs as and whether it has elevated privileges.

in_scope: Local enumeration of Snapped (10.129.9.2) as jonathan
Writable files/directories owned by root or other users
Installed software versions for known local exploits
Network services listening on localhost only
Home directories, config files, SSH keys
Nginx UI configuration and database at /var/lib/nginx-ui/ or similar
Group memberships and interesting file ACLs
out_of_scope: Other hosts on the network. Do not pivot or scan beyond 10.129.9.2.
allowed_actions: List processes, services, cron jobs, timers
Check writable paths and interesting permissions
Check kernel version and installed packages
disallowed_actions: Do not attempt exploitation or privilege escalation
Do not modify any files on the target
Do not upload tools or scripts
Do not create new users or modify accounts
completion_criteria: Running services and their users identified
Nginx UI process user and config location confirmed
All privesc leads documented in noire_findings.md and memoria
return_conditions: All enumeration complete — return with findings
Access lost — report last known state
Critical finding discovered — return immediately with details
notes: SSH creds: jonathan / linkinpark. Nginx UI v2.3.2 is running on port 9000 (proxied via nginx on 80). It could NOT write to /etc/nginx/ when we tried via the API — check what user it runs as and whether it has any special privileges. The admin bcrypt hash was NOT cracked — only jonathan's was. Check if there are other users on the system.
